title: [Fixes: #27571] Fix 'shouldResolveWinPaths' returning 'false' on Windows
body:

Fixes #27571

comments.body:
Then, maybe, you have any other idea of what caused the issue?

Because `GenVolumeMounts` returns `error` containing `volumeFormatErr` if and only if `SplitVolumeString` returns more than 3 parts. And that happens - at least, in my case - only when `shouldResolveWinPaths` returns `false`: https://play.golang.com/p/P1SrVsUcW6e
Are you using hyperV instead of wsl? If you look into shouldResolveWinPaths() it seem to only match wsl so I guess it would need to check for hyperv as well.

Yes, my Machine uses Hyper-V as its backend. However:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fac259ef9c45d45597f9ae554b221fc5b55935b6/pkg/specgen/winpath_linux.go#L8-L9

The line you mentioned is in `winpath_linux.go`, which shouldn't have been compiled for Windows. Am I wrong?

For Windows, `shouldResolveWinPaths` should unconditionally return `true`:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/fac259ef9c45d45597f9ae554b221fc5b55935b6/pkg/specgen/winpath_windows.go#L14-L15

> The line you mentioned is in winpath_linux.go, which shouldn't have been compiled for Windows. Am I wrong?

The error you are seeing is coming from the server most likely. From the error you posted in the issue it seems you are using docker-compose (which `podman compose` can call, yes it is different from `podman-compose`)
Docker sends the windows path to the server as it seems and then podman on linux must translate it to the actual linux path I guess.
Is it possible to restart failed checks? Or I'm supposed to just amend+push until it's all green?
